name: River KKK-2-4, quads possible, boat-heavy
description: Trips on the flop + two low cards on turn/river. Every hand with a K makes quads (AKs and AKo). Pocket pairs in the hole make full houses: 22 is Ks-full-of-2s, 44 is Ks-full-of-4s, QQ is Ks-full-of-Qs, AA is Ks-full-of-As. Non-pair unpaired hands (AQ, AJ, AT) are trips-on-board playing the kicker — still beaten by any full house. Tests the solver's board-derived-made-hand reasoning. Ranges use rank-based notation.

input.hero_range: QQ, AA, AKs, AKo, AQs, AQo, AJs, AJo, ATs, ATo, 22, 44
input.villain_range: QQ, AA, AKs, AKo, AQs, AQo, AJs, AJo, ATs, ATo, 22, 44

expected_notes: Populated by differential run. Quads (AKs/AKo) > all full houses (AA, QQ, 44, 22) > trips+kicker (AQ, AJ, AT). Symmetric ranges isolate the made-hand-tier handling; if hero and villain converge to materially different strategies it's likely a bug.
